# deploy

<!-- archie:ai-start -->

> Kubernetes deployment artefacts for OpenMeter: two independently versioned Helm charts (openmeter, benthos-collector) published to GHCR OCI registry, plus a local kind cluster config for development. deploy/charts owns the shared helm-docs generation pipeline used by both charts.

## Patterns

**helm-docs via shared template** — Both chart READMEs are generated by helm-docs from deploy/charts/template.md and each chart's values.yaml comments. Never hand-edit a chart README.md. (`make -C deploy/charts docs  # regenerates both chart READMEs`)
**OCI Helm registry distribution** — Charts are packaged and pushed to ghcr.io OCI registry on release tags, not a traditional index-based repository. (`helm package deploy/charts/openmeter --version <VERSION> --destination build/helm`)

## Key Files

| File | Role | Watch For |
|------|------|-----------|
| `deploy/charts/Makefile` | Drives helm-docs README generation for both charts. | Adding a third chart requires a new README.tmpl.md and an update to this Makefile. |
| `deploy/charts/template.md` | Shared helm-docs template consumed by both child charts. | Changes here affect both chart READMEs. Keep template generic. |
| `deploy/kind.yaml` | kind cluster config for local Kubernetes development with ingress port mappings. | Port mappings (80/443) must match the Helm chart service/ingress defaults. |

## Anti-Patterns

- Hand-editing a child chart README.md — it is generated by helm-docs from template.md and values.yaml comments
- Adding a third chart without a corresponding README.tmpl.md and Makefile update
- Storing shared Kubernetes manifests (CRDs, cluster-scoped RBAC) at the deploy/charts level — each chart must be self-contained
- Publishing charts without aligning Chart.yaml appVersion with the container image tag in values.yaml

## Decisions

- **Shared template.md at deploy/charts level rather than duplicated per chart.** — Avoids README format drift between the two charts; single edit point for documentation structure.
- **OCI registry (ghcr.io) as the distribution channel, not a traditional Helm repository index.** — OCI registries provide content-addressable artefact storage and align with the container image distribution model already used for the application images.

<!-- archie:ai-end -->
